label: plain_text
text:
Consider
the quadratic equation
2x² - 3x + 1 = 0. If
we
replace x
by
on
the
LHS of this
equation, we
get (2 × 12) - (3 × 1) + 1 = 0 = RHS of the equation.
We say that 1 is a root of the quadratic equation 2x2 - 3x + 1 = 0. This also means
that
1 is a zero of the quadratic polynomial 2x² - 3x + 1.
